heading: National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration satellite and data management

children.heading: Short-term management of environmental observations
children.label: a Short-term management of environmental observations
i
children.text: by deploying constellations of microsatellites in both the equatorial and polar orbits;

children.label: ii
children.text: by integrating the resulting data and research into all national operational and research weather forecast models; and

children.label: iii
children.text: by ensuring that the resulting data of National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration’s COSMIC–1 and COSMIC–2 programs are free and open to all communities.

children.heading: Annual reports
children.label: B Annual reports
children.text: Not less frequently than once each year until the Under Secretary has completed and operationalized the program described in subparagraph (A) pursuant to such subparagraph, the Under Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the status of the efforts of the Under Secretary to carry out such subparagraph.

children.heading: Integration of ocean and coastal data from the Integrated Ocean Observing System
children.label: 2 Integration of ocean and coastal data from the Integrated Ocean Observing System
A
children.text: integrate additional coastal and ocean observations, and other data and research, from the Integrated Ocean Observing System (IOOS) into regional weather forecasts to improve weather forecasts and forecasting decision support systems;

children.label: B
children.text: support the development of real-time data sharing products and forecast products in collaboration with the regional associations of such system, including contributions from the private sector, academia, and research institutions to ensure timely and accurate use of ocean and coastal data in regional forecasts; and

children.label: C
children.text: support increasing use of autonomous, mobile surface, sub-surface, and submarine vehicle ocean and fresh water sensor systems and the infrastructure necessary to share and analyze these data in real-time and feed them into predictive early warning systems.

children.heading: Existing monitoring and observation-capability
children.label: 3 Existing monitoring and observation-capability
children.text: The Under Secretary shall identify degradation of existing monitoring and observation capabilities that could lead to a reduction in forecast quality.

children.heading: Specifications for new satellite systems or data determined by operational needs
children.label: 4 Specifications for new satellite systems or data determined by operational needs
children.text: In developing specifications for any satellite systems or data to follow the Joint Polar Satellite System, Geostationary Operational Environmental Satellites, and any other satellites, in effect on the day before , the Under Secretary shall ensure the specifications are determined to the extent practicable by the recommendations of the reports under subsection (b) of this section.April 18, 2017

children.heading: Independent Study on Future of National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration satellite systems and data
children.label: b Independent Study on Future of National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration satellite systems and data

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Under Secretary shall seek to enter into an agreement with the National Academy of Sciences to perform the services covered by this subsection.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: B Timing
children.text: The Under Secretary shall seek to enter into the agreement described in subparagraph (A) before .September 30, 2018

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Under an agreement between the Under Secretary and the National Academy of Sciences under this subsection, the National Academy of Sciences shall conduct a study on matters concerning future satellite data needs.

children.label: i
children.text: develop recommendations on how to make the data portfolio of the Administration more robust and cost-effective;

children.label: ii
children.text: assess the costs and benefits of moving toward a constellation of many small satellites, standardizing satellite bus design, relying more on the purchasing of data, or acquiring data from other sources or methods;

children.label: iii
children.text: identify the environmental observations that are essential to the performance of weather models, based on an assessment of Federal, academic, and private sector weather research, and the cost of obtaining the environmental data;

children.label: iv
children.text: identify environmental observations that improve the quality of operational and research weather models in effect on the day before ;April 18, 2017

children.label: v
children.text: identify and prioritize new environmental observations that could contribute to existing and future weather models; and

children.label: vi
children.text: develop recommendations on a portfolio of environmental observations that balances essential, quality-improving, and new data, private and nonprivate sources, and space-based and Earth-based sources.

children.heading: Deadline and report
children.label: C Deadline and report
children.text: In carrying out the study under subparagraph (A), the National Academy of Sciences shall complete and transmit to the Under Secretary a report containing the findings of the National Academy of Sciences with respect to the study not later than 2 years after the date on which the Administrator enters into an agreement with the National Academy of Sciences under paragraph (1)(A).

children.label: i
children.text: is not part of the Federal Government;

children.label: ii
children.text: operates as a not-for-profit entity; and

children.label: iii
children.text: has expertise and objectivity comparable to that of the National Academy of Sciences.

children.heading: Treatment
children.label: B Treatment
children.text: If the Under Secretary enters into an agreement with another organization as described in subparagraph (A), any reference in this subsection to the National Academy of Sciences shall be treated as a reference to the other organization.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated, out of funds appropriated to National Environmental Satellite, Data, and Information Service, to carry out this subsection $1,000,000 for the period encompassing fiscal years 2018 through 2019.1So in original. Probably should be preceded by “the”.1

children.label: A
children.text: lower the cost of observations used to meet the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration’s mission requirements;

children.label: B
children.text: disaggregate current satellite systems, where appropriate;

children.label: C
children.text: include new, value-adding technological advancements; and

children.label: i
children.text: weather and climate forecasting and predictions; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the understanding, management, and exploration of the ocean.

children.label: A
children.text: may partner with the commercial and academic sectors, non-governmental and not-for-profit organizations, and other Federal agencies; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall, consistent with , undertake quantitative assessments for objective analyses, as the Under Secretary considers appropriate, to evaluate relative value and benefits of future data sources and satellite architectures described in paragraph (1).section 8517 of this title

children.heading: Additional forms of transaction authorized
children.label: d Additional forms of transaction authorized
A
children.text: basic, applied, and advanced research projects and ocean exploration missions to meet the objectives described in subparagraphs (A) through (D) of subsection (c)(1); or

children.label: B
children.text: any other type of project to meet other mission objectives, as determined by the Under Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A transaction agreement under paragraph (1) shall be limited to research and development activities.

children.label: i
children.text: for the construction, use, operation, or procurement of new, improved, innovative, or value-adding systems, including satellites, instrumentation, ground stations, data, and data processing;

children.label: ii
children.text: to make determinations on how to best use existing or planned data, systems, and assets of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration; and

children.label: iii
children.text: only when the objectives of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration cannot be met using a cooperative research and development agreement, grants procurement contract, or cooperative agreement.

children.heading: Termination of effectiveness
children.label: 3 Termination of effectiveness
children.text: The authority provided in this subsection terminates effective .September 30, 2030

children.heading: Transparency
children.label: e Transparency
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date that a transaction agreement is made under subsection (d), the Under Secretary shall make publicly available, in a searchable format, on the website of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration all uses of the authority under subsection (d), including an estimate of committed National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration resources and the expected benefits to National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration objectives for the transaction agreement, with appropriate redactions for proprietary, sensitive, or classified information.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after September 30 of each fiscal year through , the Under Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives a report on the use of additional transaction authority by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration during the previous fiscal year.September 30, 2023

children.label: i
children.text: an indication of whether the transaction agreement is a reimbursable, non-reimbursable, or funded agreement;

children.label: I
children.text: the subject and terms;

children.label: II
children.text: the parties;

children.label: III
children.text: the responsible National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration line office;

children.label: IV
children.text: the value;

children.label: V
children.text: the extent of the cost sharing among Federal Government and non-Federal sources;

children.label: VI
children.text: the duration or schedule; and

children.label: VII
children.text: all milestones;

children.label: iii
children.text: an indication of whether the transaction agreement was renewed during the previous fiscal year;

children.label: iv
children.text: the technology areas in which research projects were conducted under that agreement;

children.label: I
children.text: has contributed to a broadening of the technology and industrial base available for meeting National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration needs; and

children.label: II
children.text: has fostered within the technology and industrial base new relationships and practices that support the United States; and

children.label: vi
children.text: the total value received by the Federal Government under that agreement for that fiscal year; and

children.label: B
children.text: a list of all anticipated reimbursable, non-reimbursable, and funded transaction agreements for the upcoming fiscal year.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: g Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed as limiting the authority of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration to use cooperative research and development agreements, grants, procurement contracts, or cooperative agreements.
